{"metadata":{"kernelspec":{"display_name":"R","language":"R","name":"ir"},"language_info":{"mimetype":"text/x-r-source","name":"R","pygments_lexer":"r","version":"3.4.2","file_extension":".r","codemirror_mode":"r"},"kaggle":{"accelerator":"none","dataSources":[{"sourceType":"competition","sourceId":125220,"databundleVersionId":16144599}],"isInternetEnabled":true,"language":"rmarkdown","sourceType":"script","isGpuEnabled":false}},"nbformat_minor":4,"nbformat":4,"cells":[{"cell_type":"code","source":"---\ntitle: \"Irrigation Need\"\ndate: '`r Sys.Date()`'\noutput:\n  html_document:\n    number_sections: true\n    fig_caption: true\n    toc: true\n    fig_width: 7\n    fig_height: 4.5\n    theme: cosmo\n    highlight: tango\n    code_folding: hide\n---\n\n![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/e7de6c_05f2079e210144919dd7c330c7d1761b~mv2.gif)\n\n# Introduction  {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}\n\nThe goal of this competition is to predict irrigation needs.\n\nMy notebook serves as a demonstration of some of the possible techniques available to arrive at a solution.  I intend to add to this as I have time available. Your questions and comments are welcome.\n\nLets dive right in.\n\nThe Kaggle kernels have many of the common r packages built in.  \n\n## Load libraries\n\n```{r }\n#| label: setup\n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\n\nif (dir.exists(\"/kaggle\")){\n  path <- \"/kaggle/input/competitions/playground-series-s6e4/\"\n# options(repos = c(CRAN = \"https://packagemanager.posit.co/cran/2021-03-22\"))\n\ninstall.packages(\"pak\", quiet = TRUE)\npak::pak(\"tidymodels/bonsai\")\n# pak::pak(\"ggplot2\")\n\nremotes::install_github(\"luisdva/hexsession\", quiet = TRUE)    \n\ncores <- future::availableCores()\n\n} else {\n  path <- stringr::str_c(here::here(\"data\"),\"/\")\n  orig_path <- stringr::str_c(here::here(\"data\"),\"/\")\n\n  cores <- future::availableCores(omit = 2)\n  \n}\n \nsuppressPackageStartupMessages({\nlibrary(tidyverse, quietly = TRUE) # metapackage of all tidyverse packages\nlibrary(tidymodels, quietly = TRUE) # metapackage see https://www.tidymodels.org/\n\nlibrary(correlationfunnel)\n\nlibrary(bonsai)\n \nlibrary(DALEXtra) # Tidymodels Explainability         \n\n    \n})\n\nconflicted::conflict_prefer(\"select\", \"dplyr\")\nconflicted::conflict_prefer(\"where\", \"dplyr\")\n\ntidymodels_prefer()\n\noptions(tidymodels.dark = TRUE)\n\ntheme_set(theme_minimal())\n\nhexsession::make_tile(packages = c(\"bonsai\", \"DALEXtra\", \"DALEX\",  \"correlationfunnel\",\"yardstick\",\"workflows\",\n                                   \"tune\",\"rsample\",\"recipes\",\"parsnip\",\"dials\",\"broom\",\"tidymodels\",\n                                   \"forcats\",\"dplyr\",\"readr\",\"tidyr\",\"ggplot2\",\"tidyverse\"),\n                   #  highlight_mode = TRUE\n                     )\n\n\n```\n\n\n\n## Load Data\n\n```{r }\n#| label: load data\n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\npreprocessor <- function(dataframe) {\n\ndataframe <- dataframe |>\n    janitor::clean_names() |>     \n  \n  mutate(across(c(where(is.character)), \\(x) as.factor(x)))  \n\nreturn(dataframe)\n}\n\nraw_df <- read_csv(str_c(path, \"train.csv\"),\n                   show_col_types = FALSE) \n\ntst_df <- read_csv(str_c(path, \"test.csv\"),\n                   show_col_types = FALSE)  \n\nall_df <-\n    bind_rows(raw_df %>% mutate(source = \"train\"),\n              tst_df %>% mutate(source = \"test\")) |>\n          preprocessor() |> \n  mutate(irrigation_need = factor(irrigation_need))\n\ncluster_fit64 <- all_df |> \n  select(where(is.numeric), -id, -irrigation_need) |> \n  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), scale)) |> \n  kmeans(centers = 64)\n\n\nall_df <- all_df |> \n  bind_cols(tibble(cluster64 = factor(fitted(cluster_fit64, method = \"classes\")))) |> \n  mutate(wt = if_else(source == \"test\",1,0)) |> \n  mutate(cluster_test_count = sum(wt), .by = cluster64) |> \n  mutate(weight = importance_weights((cluster_test_count + 0.1 ))) |> \n  select( -cluster_test_count,-wt)\n\ntrain_df <- all_df |>  \n  dplyr::filter(source == \"train\") |>  \n  dplyr::select(-source) \n\ncompetition_df <- all_df |>  \n  dplyr::filter(source == \"test\") |>  \n  dplyr::select(-source, -irrigation_need)\n\n\n```\n\n\n\n\n## Features\n\n```{r}\n#| label: Features\n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\n\n\n\nfeatures <- train_df %>%\n  dplyr::select(-id, -irrigation_need, -weight) |> \n  names()\n\n#train_df <- train_df %>% \n#  dplyr::group_by(pick({{features}})) |> \n#  dplyr::summarise(id = min(id),\n#                   irrigation_need = first(irrigation_need),\n#                   .groups = \"drop\")\n\nnom_features <- train_df |> \n  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(features)) |> \n  dplyr::select(dplyr::where(is.factor)) |> \n  names() \n\nlogical_features <- train_df |> \n  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(features)) |> \n  dplyr::select(dplyr::where(is.logical)) |> \n  names() \n\nnum_features <- train_df |> \n  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(features)) |> \n  dplyr::select(dplyr::where(is.numeric)) |> \n  names()\n```\n\nNominal features:\n\n`r nom_features`\n\nNumeric features: \n\n`r num_features`\n\nLogical features: \n\n`r logical_features`\n\n\nSize of the combined train and competition datasets:\n\n`r nrow(all_df)`\n\nSize of training set after de-duplication\n\n`r nrow(train_df)`\n\n\n\n# EDA {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}\n\n## Numeric features\n\n\n```{r}\n#| label: numeric\n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\n#| fig.height: 9\n\ntrain_df %>% \n  dplyr::select(all_of(num_features), irrigation_need ) %>% \n  pivot_longer(-irrigation_need,\n    names_to = \"metric\",\n    values_to = \"value\"\n  ) %>%\n  ggplot(aes(value, fill = irrigation_need)) +\n  geom_density(position = \"stack\") +\n  scale_fill_brewer(type = \"qual\", palette = \"Dark2\") +        \n  facet_wrap(vars(metric), scales = \"free\", ncol = 3) +\n  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),\n        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),\n        legend.position = \"top\") +\n  labs(color = NULL, fill = \"irrigation_need\",\n       title = \"Numeric Feature Univariate Distributions\",\n       caption = \"Data: Kaggle | Visual: Jim Gruman\")\n\n\n```\n\n\n## Nominal and Logical features\n\nExplore the distribution of outcome class by factor level.\n\n\n```{r}\n#| label: nominal\n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\n#| fig-height: 9\n\nif(length(nom_features) >0){\n\ntrain_df %>% \n  select(all_of(nom_features), all_of(logical_features), irrigation_need) %>% \n  mutate(across(nom_features, fct_lump_n, n = 5, other_level = 'other', ties.method = \"first\"),\n         across(logical_features, \\(x) factor(x))) %>%\n  pivot_longer(-irrigation_need,\n    names_to = \"metric\",\n    values_to = \"value\"\n  ) %>%\n    \n  filter(!is.na(irrigation_need)) %>% \n    \n  summarise(n = n(),\n            .by = c(irrigation_need, metric, value)) %>%\n      \n  mutate(value = tidytext::reorder_within(value, n, metric)) %>%\n    \n  ggplot(aes(x = n, y = value, fill = irrigation_need)) +\n  geom_col() +\n  tidytext::scale_y_reordered() +\n  scale_x_continuous(n.breaks = 3, guide = guide_axis(n.dodge = 2))  +\n  scale_fill_brewer(type = \"qual\", palette = \"Dark2\") +        \n  facet_wrap(vars(metric), scales = \"free\", ncol = 2) +\n  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),\n        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),\n  #  strip.text = element_text(margin = margin_part(b = -20, l = 8)),\n  #  strip.clip = \"off\",\n  #  panel.spacing = unit(2, \"lines\"),\n       legend.position = \"bottom\") +\n  labs(title = \"Nominal Feature Counts\",\n       fill = NULL, y = NULL,\n       caption = \"Data: Kaggle | Visual: Jim Gruman\")\n\n}\n\n```\n\n## Counts of Missingness\n\n                  \n```{r}\n#| label: counts of missingness\n\ntrain_df %>% \n  summarize(across(all_of(features), function(x) sum(is.na(x)))) %>% \n  pivot_longer(everything(),\n              names_to = \"feature\",\n              values_to = \"Count of Missing\") %>% \n                   knitr::kable()\n\ncompetition_df %>% \n  summarize(across(all_of(features), function(x) sum(is.na(x)))) %>% \n  pivot_longer(everything(),\n              names_to = \"feature\",\n              values_to = \"Count of Missing\") %>% \n                   knitr::kable()\n\n```\n\n## Counts of Distinct\n                   \n               \n```{r}\n#| label: counts of distinct\n               \ntrain_df %>%\n  summarize(across(all_of(features), n_distinct)) %>%\n  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = \"feature\", values_to = \"Count of distinct train\") |>\n  left_join(\n    competition_df %>%\n      summarize(across(all_of(features), n_distinct)) %>%\n      pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = \"feature\", values_to = \"Count of distinct test\"),\n    by = join_by(feature)\n  ) %>% \n                   knitr::kable()\n               \n```\n\n## Duplicated\n\nIs this competition transaction already in the training data with a correct irrigation_need?\n\n\n```{r}\n#| label: duplicates\n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\n\nbind_rows(train_df %>% mutate(source = \"train\"),\n              competition_df %>% mutate(source = \"test\")) |> \n    group_by_at(features) %>%\n    mutate(num_dups = n(),\n           dup_id = row_number()) %>% \n    ungroup() %>% \n    group_by(source) %>%\n    mutate(is_duplicated = dup_id > 1) %>% \n    count(is_duplicated) %>% \n                   knitr::kable()\n\n\n```\n\n\n## Pairwise Correlations\n                   \n`ggcorrplot` provides a quick look at numeric features where the correlation may be significant. \n\n```{r}\n#| label: pairwise correlations\n#| fig-width: 16\n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\n                   \n\ntrainp.mat <- train_df %>% \n  mutate(irrigation_need = as.numeric(irrigation_need)) |> \n  select(all_of(num_features), irrigation_need) %>% \n  ggcorrplot::cor_pmat() \n                   \ntrain_df %>% \n  mutate(irrigation_need = as.numeric(irrigation_need)) |> \n  select(all_of(num_features), irrigation_need) %>%                    \n  cor() %>% \n  ggcorrplot::ggcorrplot(hc.order = TRUE, \n                         lab = TRUE,\n                         p.mat = trainp.mat,\n    type = \"lower\", insig = \"blank\") +\n  labs(title = \"Pairwise Correlations Training Set\")\n                   \ncompetitionp.mat <- competition_df %>% \n  select(all_of(num_features)) %>% \n  ggcorrplot::cor_pmat() \n                   \ncompetition_df %>% \n  select(all_of(num_features)) %>%                   \n  cor()   %>% \n  ggcorrplot::ggcorrplot(hc.order = TRUE, lab = TRUE,\n                         p.mat = competitionp.mat,\n    type = \"lower\", insig = \"blank\") +\n   labs(title = \"Pairwise Correlations Competition Set\")\n\n```                    \n\n## Correlation Funnel\n\n\n```{r}\n#| label: correlation funnel \n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\n\n\ny_df <- train_df |>\n  transmute(irrigation_need = as.numeric(irrigation_need)) \n\ntrain_df %>%\n  select(all_of(features)) %>%\n  correlationfunnel::binarize(one_hot = TRUE, n_bins = 3) %>%\n  bind_cols(y_df) |>\n  correlate(target = irrigation_need) %>%\n  plot_correlation_funnel()\n\n``` \n                   \n                   \n## Target\n\n```{r}\n#| label: outcome \n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\n#| fig.width: 6\n\ntrain_df %>%\n  summarize(outcome_sum = n(), .by = irrigation_need) %>%\n  arrange(outcome_sum) %>%\n  mutate(prop = outcome_sum / nrow(train_df)) %>%\n  mutate(ypos = cumsum(prop) - 0.5 * prop) %>%\n  ggplot(aes(x = \"\", y = prop, fill = irrigation_need)) +\n  geom_bar(stat = \"identity\",\n           width = 1,\n           show.legend = FALSE) +\n  geom_text(\n    aes(\n      y = ypos,\n      label = paste0(irrigation_need\n                     , \"\\n\", round(prop, 2) * 100, \"%\")\n    ),\n    color = \"white\",\n    nudge_x = 0,\n    size = 3\n  ) +\n  scale_fill_brewer(type = \"qual\", palette = \"Dark2\") +                   \n  coord_polar(\"y\", start = 0) +\n  theme(\n    axis.text = element_blank(),\n    axis.title = element_blank()  ) +\n  theme_void() +\n  labs(title = \"Irrigation Need Label\", caption = \"Data: Kaggle.com | Visual: Jim Gruman\")\n\n``` \n                \n# Machine Learning {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}\n\n\n## Recipe\n\n```{r}\n#| label: recipe\n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\n#| fig.width: 12\n\n   \nrec <- recipe(irrigation_need ~ ., data = train_df) |> \n  update_role(id, new_role = \"id variable\") |> \n  step_nzv(all_predictors()) |> \n  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors())     \n\nfolds <- vfold_cv(train_df,\n                  v = 5,\n                  repeats = 1,\n                  strata = irrigation_need)\nfolds |>\n  tidy() |>\n  ggplot(aes(x = Fold, y = factor(Row), fill = Data)) +\n  geom_tile() +\n  scale_fill_brewer(type = \"qual\", palette = \"Dark2\") +        \n  labs(y = \"Data Point\") +\n  theme(\n    axis.text.y = element_blank(),\n    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()\n  )                        \n                   \n```\n\n\n## GBMs\n\n\n```{r}\n#| label: ml \n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\n\nlightgbm_spec <- boost_tree(\n  trees = 1300,\n  tree_depth = tune(),\n  learn_rate = tune(),\n  min_n = tune()) |>\n  set_engine(engine = \"lightgbm\",\n             early_stopping_round = 40,\n#             stop_iter = 5,\n             num_threads = cores,\n             num_leaves = tune()) |>\n  set_mode(mode = \"classification\")\n\nlgbm_wflow <- workflow() |> \n  add_case_weights(weight) |> \n  add_recipe(rec) |> \n  add_model(lightgbm_spec)\n                 \ngrid <- lgbm_wflow |>\n extract_parameter_set_dials() |>\n update( num_leaves = num_leaves(range = c(50,100)),\n         min_n = min_n(range = c(4,30)),\n         tree_depth = tree_depth(range = c(8, 15)),\n         learn_rate = learn_rate(range = c(-1.7,-0.9))\n ) |> \n grid_latin_hypercube(size = 15)                   \n                   \nall_models <- tune_grid(\n          lgbm_wflow,\n          folds,\n          grid = grid,\n          metrics = metric_set(f_meas, bal_accuracy),\n          control = control_grid()\n          )\n\nall_models %>%\n  collect_metrics() |>\n  arrange(desc(mean))\n\nall_models |>\n  autoplot() +\n  labs(title = \"Lightgbm Hyperparameter Search\")\n\nbest_params <- all_models |> \n  select_best(metric = \"bal_accuracy\")  \n\nclassification_fit <- \n  workflow(rec, lightgbm_spec) |> \n  finalize_workflow(best_params) %>%\n  fit(train_df)   \n\nclassification_fit %>%\n   extract_fit_parsnip() %>%\n   vip::vip(num_features = 25) +\n   labs(title = \"LightGBM Permutation Variable Importance\")\n                   \n\n```\n\n\n\n       \n\n\n                   \n# Submission\n                   \n\n```{r}\n#| label: submission\n#| warning: false\n#| message: false\n#| fig.height: 6\n#| fig.width: 6   \n\n                 \npredict(classification_fit, train_df) %>% \n  bind_cols(train_df) |> \n  conf_mat(irrigation_need, .pred_class) %>%\n  yardstick:::autoplot.conf_mat(type = \"heatmap\") +\n  scale_fill_distiller(palette = \"RdPu\")\n\npredict(classification_fit, train_df) %>% \n  bind_cols(train_df) |> \n  yardstick::bal_accuracy(irrigation_need, .pred_class)\n                   \nsubmit_df <- predict(classification_fit, competition_df) |>\n  bind_cols(competition_df) |>\n\n  transmute(\n   id,\n  `Irrigation_Need` = .pred_class)\n\nhead(submit_df)  %>% \n     knitr::kable()      \n\nsubmit_df  %>% \n  write_csv(\"submission.csv\")\n``` ","metadata":{"_uuid":"dec978c2-d73f-44a6-acd7-97b1d246d1ba","_cell_guid":"2d7fa95e-2108-462e-9470-d4211c85cd53","trusted":true,"collapsed":false,"jupyter":{"outputs_hidden":false}},"outputs":[],"execution_count":null}]}
